heading: Art. 58b. Sentences: forfeiture of pay and allowances during confinement
label: 858b Art. 58b. Sentences: forfeiture of pay and allowances during confinement
notes: Amendments2017—Subsec. (b). substituted “section 860a or 860b of this title (article 60a or 60b)” for “ (article 60)”.Pub. L. 115–91section 860 of this title2016—Subsec. children.text: A court-martial sentence described in paragraph (2) shall result in the forfeiture of pay, or of pay and allowances, due that member during any period of confinement or parole. The forfeiture pursuant to this section shall take effect on the date determined under (article 57) and may be deferred as provided in that section. The pay and allowances forfeited, in the case of a general court-martial, shall be all pay and allowances due that member during such period and, in the case of a special court-martial, shall be two-thirds of all pay due that member during such period.section 857 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: confinement for more than six months or death; or

children.label: B
children.text: confinement for six months or less and a dishonorable or bad-conduct discharge or dismissal.

children.label: b
children.text: In a case involving an accused who has dependents, the convening authority or other person acting under section 860a or 860b of this title (article 60a or 60b) may waive any or all of the forfeitures of pay and allowances required by subsection (a) for a period not to exceed six months. Any amount of pay or allowances that, except for a waiver under this subsection, would be forfeited shall be paid, as the convening authority or other person taking action directs, to the dependents of the accused.

children.label: c
children.text: If the sentence of a member who forfeits pay and allowances under subsection (a) is set aside or disapproved or, as finally approved, does not provide for a punishment referred to in subsection (a)(2), the member shall be paid the pay and allowances which the member would have been paid, except for the forfeiture, for the period during which the forfeiture was in effect.
